description: Lac operon in KEGG style: gene orthologs (eco:b), lac promoter, allolactose, and pathway (lactose degradation to glucose + galactose). Regulation: LacI represses, CRP activates promoter; allolactose relieves LacI.
name: Lac Operon (KEGG-sourced)
notes: V5 = KEGG only. KEGG GENES (eco), lactose/galactose pathway, allolactose, lac promoter; LacI/CRP regulatory links.
mermaid:
graph TD
    LAC[Lactose]
    LACI[eco:b1677 lacI]
    CRP[eco:b3356 crp]
    ALLO[Allolactose]
    PROM[lac promoter]
    LACZ[eco:b1675 lacZ]
    LACY[eco:b1676 lacY]
    LACA[eco:b1674 lacA]
    GAL[Galactose]
    GLU[Glucose]
    LAC -->|lacY transport| LACY
    LAC -->|lacZ isomerase| ALLO
    ALLO -->|inducer| LACI
    LACI -->|repress| PROM
    CRP -->|activate| PROM
    PROM --> LACZ
    PROM --> LACY
    PROM --> LACA
    LACZ -->|hydrolysis| GAL
    LACZ -->|hydrolysis| GLU
    style LAC fill:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style LACI fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style CRP fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style ALLO fill:#74c0fc,color:#000
    style PROM fill:#51cf66,color:#000
    style LACZ fill:#74c0fc,color:#000
    style LACY fill:#74c0fc,color:#000
    style LACA fill:#74c0fc,color:#000
    style GAL fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style GLU fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
scientificAccuracy: KEGG pathway and gene annotations; lac promoter and allolactose from lactose metabolism map.

sources.title: KEGG
sources.note: KEGG GENES (eco), lactose pathway, lac regulatory links.
